title: Directive applied-residue / region-annotation marker grammar — the reserved, matcher-excluded sigil for machine-emitted region boundaries vs authored directive nodes
summary: Prep research for decision #1989 (raised by #1986's resolved family-invariant rule 5; we:reports/2026-06-30-directive-residue-marker-grammar.md). The family invariant requires authored directive nodes and the region/residue annotations a directive LEAVES when applied to occupy DISJOINT, matcher-excluded grammars — every registry's upgrade walk claims only authored nodes (residue never re-upgraded) and the two read apart in raw DOM/devtools. #1989 picks the ONE reserved residue sigil, unifying today's two ad-hoc conventions. CODE GROUND TRUTH (frontierui): the family emits TWO boundary conventions today — (1) machine residue `<!-- for-each:start (@users) -->` … `<!-- for-each:end -->`, emitted by runtime behaviors (fui:blocks/for-each/ForEachBehavior.ts:105-106, fui:blocks/view/ViewIfDirective.ts:56-57, fui:blocks/view/ViewSwitchDirective.ts:62-63); and (2) authored open/close `<!-- control:if -->` … `<!-- /control:if -->` (fui:plugs/webdirectives/CustomCommentParser.ts:60-73). The matcher-exclusion seed is leading-`/` only: directiveNameOf returns null for `/`-prefixed (fui:plugs/webdirectives/CustomCommentRegistry.ts:39-43), as do DefaultCommentParser.parse and multiTemplate.openingDirectiveName. The inspector (#1973) reads BOTH and reconstructs a nested region tree labelled by directive name + bound-state expression (fui:plugs/webdirectives/directiveInspector.ts:88-150). LATENT GAP (the concrete reason this isn't cosmetic): a residue OPEN marker token `for-each:start` matches multiTemplate's opening-directive regex /^[\w-]+:[\w-]+$/ (for-each : start) — and so does `for-each:end`; neither starts with `/`, so the leading-`/` seed does NOT exclude them. They are safe today only because no registered directive happens to be named `for-each:start` — disjointness by LUCK (absence of collision), not by grammar. PRIOR ART (machine-emitted DOM boundary comments; native HTML reserves no comment grammar, so the platform shape is the cross-framework de-facto convention, strikingly consistent): React Suspense `<!--$-->`/`<!--/$-->` (+ `$!` fallback, `$?` pending) — paired, positional, `/`-close echoes HTML end-tag (React DOM config source). Vue 3 fragment anchors `<!--[-->`/`<!--]-->` + empty `<!---->` v-if anchor — bracket pair, positional (vue runtime-core/hydration.ts). Svelte 5 `<!--[-->`/`<!--]-->` with state suffixes `<!--[!-->` (else) / `<!--[?-->` (hydration-failed) — bracket pair, positional (svelte constants.js). lit-html `<!--?lit$NONCE$-->` — per-render random nonce payload (lit-html.ts). Solid `<!--$-->`/`<!--/-->` depth-counted — notably MIGRATED `#`→`$` because `#`-leading comments collide with server/CDN SSI directives (dom-expressions client.js). Angular `<!--container-->`/`<!--bindings={...}-->` — the one mainstream scheme that embeds a human-readable LABEL + dev-mode reflected state. CROSS-CUTTING: (a) every framework leads with a non-alphanumeric sigil authored content never starts with, so detection is an O(1) data[0] test; (b) paired block boundaries are overwhelmingly BRACKET-based (Vue + Svelte independently); (c) `/`-close echoes the HTML end-tag (the same `/` FUI already excludes); (d) markers are mostly anonymous-positional EXCEPT Angular, which carries readable labels — so FUI's name+expr payload has mainstream precedent. SYNTHESIS for #1989: Fork 1 — adopt the bracket-sigil pair `<!--[name expr-->`/`<!--]name-->` (cheapest O(1) `[`/`]` exclusion, collision-free vs the authored ns:name opening-token shape, paired+balanced, closes the multiTemplate mis-claim gap); alternatives keep `:start`/`:end` (shipped but suffix-test costlier + ns:name-shaped) or a single reserved prefix char (`%`/`~`, weaker precedent). Fork 2 — keep the AUTHORED `/name` close (mirrors the native HTML end-tag shape hand-authors know; propose-standard-in-platform-shape) and use the bracket sigil only for machine residue, unifying the MATCHER behind one exclusion predicate that accepts both `/`- and `[`/`]`-prefixes — vs the purist fold of `/name` into `]name` for one literal sigil at an author-ergonomics cost. Name+expression payload is supported-by-default (not a fork): the #1973 non-invasive inspector requires it (start carries expr, end is bare). Codifies into we:docs/agent/block-standard.md#directive-registration-mechanism rule 5 (turning the delegated-sigil placeholder into the ratified grammar) AND corrects rule 5's stale delegation pointer — block-standard delegates the residue sigil to #1987, but #1987 explicitly punts it to #1989, so the pointer must read #1989.
